title: IEEE half/bfloat decode is now a Form recipe — four-way proven, bit-exact vs numpy, closing q4k-dequant's named f16->f32 rung
advances: form-native-models.form rung (1b): q4k-dequant.fk lifted the Q4_K weight unpack into Form but its super-scale d and super-min dmin entered as already-decoded f32. In a real GGUF block_q4_K those scales are stored as ggml_half (IEEE 754 half). This recipe decodes a raw 16-bit integer pattern back to its f32 value, so the dequant's scales can come straight from the file bytes — the model's weights become cells through a recipe end to end.
device: Apple M4 Max (40-core GPU), arm64 Darwin 26.3.1 — proof is host-independent (pure arithmetic band)
recipe: form/form-stdlib/f16-decode.fk — the INVERSE of format-arith.fk's quantize, ONE recipe parameterized by the format's field widths (eb exponent bits, mb mantissa bits): f16 = (5,10) bias 15, bf16 = (8,7) bias 127. Field extraction is integer arithmetic (mantissa = bits & (2^mb-1) = fd-mod; exponent = (bits >> mb) & (2^eb-1); sign = bits >> (eb+mb)), so the band proves on the four-way arithmetic floor with NO bitwise primitive. Value rebuilds by exact powers of two (format-arith's fq-pow2): normal -> (-1)^s 2^(exp-bias)(1+mant/2^mb); subnormal/zero (exp==0) -> (-1)^s 2^(1-bias)(mant/2^mb).
scope: sign + normal + subnormal + zero decoded; inf/nan (exp all-ones) out of scope (not finite, no eq verdict) — named honestly like format-arith's normal-range scope.
band: form/form-stdlib/tests/f16-decode-band.fk — 12 strange-minimal claims spanning both formats, both signs, the normal/exp<bias/subnormal/zero paths, the smallest subnormal and largest normal, AND the two REAL gguf scales: f16 bits 1780 -> d=0.00010609626770019531 and bits 5061 -> dmin=0.0009484291076660156, the same scales q4k-dequant-band.fk feeds in pre-decoded.
reference: numpy IEEE half/bfloat (np.uint16/uint32 .view) — the Form decode matches bit-exact on every case incl. min-subnormal (2^-24), max-normal (65504), zero, both signs, both formats, and the round-trip of the two real gguf scales.
proof: cd form && ./validate.sh form-stdlib/core.fk form-stdlib/format-arith.fk form-stdlib/f16-decode.fk form-stdlib/tests/f16-decode-band.fk -> 4095, 1 ok, 0 divergent — kernels agree on every sample (fourth arm fkwu crossed)
verdict: FOUR-WAY 4095, 0 divergent — Go, Rust, TypeScript, and the emitted fkwu walker all decode IEEE half/bfloat bit-for-bit against numpy, including the real llama3.2:3b Q4_K super-scale/super-min.
reading: The last external dependency in real-model weight loading — decoding the half-precision superblock scales — is now a proven Form recipe. q4k-dequant's MATH (PR #3226) plus this decode means a GGUF Q4_K superblock can be unpacked to f32 entirely in Form arithmetic; the only remaining host carrier in the load path is read_file_bytes (host I/O, by design). Remaining toward full real-model inference: the full transformer block (attention/softmax/SwiGLU) on the GPU, then the generation loop.
reproduce: venv with numpy; np.float16(d).view(np.uint16) == 1780 and np.float16(dmin).view(np.uint16) == 5061; the band fixture is those bit patterns + edge cases; validate.sh four-way == 4095
